title: Monitor for underground infrastructure
abstract: A device for monitoring an underground utility vault environment includes a waterproof housing, a sensing chamber defined at least in part by an exterior of the housing and having a cover configured to allow fluid communication between the sensing chamber and the utility vault environment, a plurality of sensors including a gas sensor for measuring a gas concentration in the utility vault environment and a water sensor for detecting the presence of water in the sensing chamber, and a communications module for transmitting sensor-related data from the device. The gas sensor and the communications module are mounted in the interior of the housing and a port allows gas exchange between the sensing chamber and the gas sensor whilst preventing water ingress from the exterior into the interior of the housing. In another embodiment, a monitoring device is configured to determine whether an alarm condition is present based on the output data from a primary sensor, and to compile and transmit an alarm data packet comprising data derived from the or each primary sensor as well as at least one secondary sensor.
claims:
Claims 1. A device for monitoring an underground utility vault environment, comprising: a waterproof housing defining an interior and an exterior; a sensing chamber defined at least in part by the exterior of the housing and having a cover configured to allow fluid communication between the sensing chamber and the utility vault environment; a plurality of sensors including a gas sensor for measuring a gas concentration in the utility vault environment and a water sensor for detecting the presence of water in the sensing chamber; and a communications module for transmitting sensor-related data from the device; wherein the gas sensor and the communications module are mounted in the interior of the housing; and wherein the device further comprises a port for allowing gas exchange between the sensing chamber and the gas sensor, the port being arranged to prevent water ingress from the exterior into the interior of the housing. 2. A device according to Claim 1 , wherein the water sensor comprises a detector responsive to contact with water, and wherein the detector is disposed in the sensing chamber. 3. A device according to Claim 2, wherein the detector comprises a pair of electrical contacts such that, in use, water bridging the pair of contacts is detectable by an increase in electrical conductivity between the contacts. 4. A device according to Claim 3, wherein the electrical contacts are disposed adjacent to the port. 5. A device according to Claim 4, wherein the contacts are annular and arranged concentrically around the port such that, in use, water bridges the 

 pair of contacts before reaching the port irrespective of the orientation of the device. 6. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the port comprises a micropermeable membrane that allows gas flow through the port and prevents water ingress through the port. 7. A device according to any of Claims 1 to 5, wherein the port comprises an aperture in the housing and a valve member moveable between an open position in which the aperture is open to allow gas flow into the interior of the housing and a closed position in which the valve member seals the aperture to prevent water ingress, and wherein the device is arranged to move the valve member to the closed position upon detection of water by the water sensor. 8. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the cover comprises a grille. 9. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the cover is arranged to guard against intrusion into the sensing chamber. 10. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the communications module is mounted on a main circuit board and the gas sensor is mounted on a daughter circuit board disposed adjacent to a wall of the sensing chamber. 11. A device according to any preceding claim, further comprising a power supply for supplying electrical power to the device, the power supply being disposed in the interior of the housing. 12. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the gas sensor comprises a carbon monoxide sensor. 13. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the sensors comprise one or more further gas sensors selected from the group consisting of a further carbon monoxide sensor, a methane sensor, a humidity sensor and a smoke sensor. 14. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the sensors comprise one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of a temperature sensor, an electrical arc sensor, a stray voltage sensor, a water sensor, a current sensor, a voltage sensor and a tamper sensor. 15. A device according to any preceding claim, wherein the sensors comprise one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of an atmospheric pressure sensor, an acceleration sensor, a light sensor, and an audio sensor. 16. A device according to any preceding claim, further comprising a camera module for acquiring images of the interior of the utility vault. 17. A device for monitoring an underground utility vault environment, including: a plurality of environmental sensors comprising at least one primary sensor and at least one secondary sensor; a processor for receiving output data from the sensors and for compiling an alarm data packet; and a communications module for transmitting data from the device; wherein the processor is configured to: - determine whether an alarm condition is present based on the output data from the or each primary sensor; - and, when it is determined that an alarm condition is present, compile an alarm data packet comprising data derived from the or each primary sensor and the or each secondary sensor, and cause the communications module to transmit the alarm data packet; and wherein the plurality of environmental sensors includes a primary sensor 

 comprising a carbon monoxide sensor. 18. A device according to Claim 17, including a further primary sensor comprising a temperature sensor. 19. A device according to Claim 17 or Claim 18, comprising one or more further primary sensors selected from the group consisting of an electrical arc sensor, a smoke sensor, a stray voltage sensor, a water sensor, and a tamper sensor. 20. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 19, comprising one or more secondary sensors selected from the group consisting of a humidity sensor, an atmospheric pressure sensor, a methane sensor, a water sensor, an acceleration sensor, a tamper sensor, a light sensor, a current sensor, a voltage sensor and an audio sensor. 21. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 20, further comprising a camera module, wherein the processor is configured to cause the camera module to acquire an image of the interior of the utility vault when it is determined that an alarm condition is present, and to transmit the acquired image. 22. A device according to Claim 21 , wherein the camera module is configured to acquire an infrared image. 23. A device according to Claim 21 or Claim 22, wherein the camera module is configured to acquire a visible light image. 24. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 23, wherein the or at least one of the secondary sensors is switchable between a sleep state in which the sensor is not operational and an awake state in which the sensor transmits output data to the processor, and wherein the processor is configured to switch the sensor from the sleep state to the awake state when it is 

 determined that an alarm condition is present. 25. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 24, wherein the or at least one of the primary sensors is switchable between a sleep state in which the sensor is not operational and an awake state in which the sensor transmits output data to the processor, and wherein the processor is configured to switch the sensor from the sleep state to the awake state at predetermined polling time intervals. 26. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 25, wherein the processor is configured to obtain data from at least one sensor at predetermined polling time intervals and to log the obtained data in a memory. 27. A device according to Claim 26, wherein the processor is configured to compile one or more reporting data packets comprising logged data and to cause the communications module to transmit the one or more reporting data packets at predetermined reporting time intervals. 28. A device according to Claim 26 or Claim 27, wherein the processor is configured to cause the communications module to transmit one or more reporting data packets upon receiving a request. 29. A device according to any of Claims 17 to 28, wherein the communications module is configured to receive firmware update data from a remote server. 30. An underground vault monitoring system, comprising a device according to any preceding claim and a receiver for communication with the device by way of a network. 31. An underground vault monitoring system according to Claim 30, comprising a plurality of devices according to any of Claims 1 to 29, each in communication with the receiver.
